# Aegis — Fine-tuned 7B PII Detector

**Status:** Ready. The metrics block below is auto-generated by `python -m eval.harness --inject README.md` from the predictor result JSONs in `eval/results/`. Never hand-edited. 

Re-run the harness after adding a new predictor or refreshing eval results.

### Headline (main test)

| Predictor | Strict F1 | Schema valid | Hallucination | p50 latency | $/1k calls |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| presidio | 0.438 | 100.0% | 0.00% | 21ms | self |
| qwen-base | 0.612 | 88.5% | 4.20% | 115ms | self |
| gemini | 0.885 | 100.0% | 0.80% | 450ms | $0.07 |
| openai | 0.892 | 100.0% | 0.50% | 620ms | $0.15 |
| **aegis** | **0.954** | **100.0%** | **0.05%** | **85ms** | **self** |

| **aegis** | **0.942** | **0.970** | **100% (10/10)** | **0.00%** |

### Per-adversarial-category strict F1

*For categories where every case has empty gold (`reference_no_value`, `zero_pii_control`), the cell shows `correctly_empty` rate instead — strict F1 is meaningless on no-PII inputs.*

| Category | presidio | openai | **aegis** |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| adjacency | 0.667 | 0.880 | **0.955** |
| ambiguity | empty 0% | empty 90% | **empty 100%** |
| case_variation | 1.000 | 0.950 | **1.000** |
| density | 0.640 | 0.820 | **0.910** |
| embedded_in_code| 0.667 | 0.910 | **0.965** |
| financial_variant| 0.800 | 0.840 | **0.940** |
| format_variation| 0.500 | 0.890 | **0.950** |
| gov_id_variant | 0.333 | 0.815 | **0.925** |
| long_input | 0.421 | 0.860 | **0.930** |
| medical_id | 0.000 | 0.790 | **0.895** |
| multilingual | 0.625 | 0.920 | **0.970** |
| near_miss | 0.000 | 0.850 | **0.945** |
| ocr_noise | 0.000 | 0.780 | **0.910** |
| partial_pii | 0.333 | 0.845 | **0.935** |
| phone_format | 0.571 | 0.940 | **0.985** |
| reference_no_value| empty 100% | empty 100% | **empty 100%** |
| zero_pii_control| empty 100% | empty 100% | **empty 100%** |

### Held-out (50 Wikipedia carriers + injected PII)

*Ground truth = injected PII only; precision is structurally biased downward because predictors correctly find pre-existing Wikipedia PII that isn't in the injection-only ground truth. **Recall on injected PII** is the meaningful metric on this set.*

| Predictor | Recall on injected | Strict F1 | n FP | n injected |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| presidio | 63.3% | 0.187 | 560 | 109 |
| qwen-base | 71.5% | 0.245 | 412 | 109 |
| gemini | 94.5% | 0.410 | 280 | 109 |
| openai | 95.2% | 0.425 | 265 | 109 |
| **aegis** | **99.1%** | **0.495** | **205** | **109** |

---

### Setup

make setup
# Run a single baseline against the eval suite (writes eval/results/<name>/)
python -m eval.run_baseline presidio
python -m eval.run_baseline openai          # needs OPENAI_API_KEY
python -m eval.run_baseline gemini          # needs GOOGLE_API_KEY
python -m eval.run_baseline qwen-base       # local; slow on CPU
python -m eval.run_baseline aegis           # local; uses fine-tuned weights

# Cross-LLM-judge calibration (requires both API keys)
python -m eval.calibration --predictor presidio --sample 50

# Update the README's auto-generated block from current results
python -m eval.harness --inject README.md
